name: Kagent
description: Kagent is a Kubernetes-native platform for AI agents specialized in DevOps and platform engineering. Uses CRDs to define agents declaratively with Microsoft AutoGen as the multi-agent runtime. Ships with pre-built tools for kubectl, Helm, Istio, Argo CD, Prometheus, and GitHub. Built by Solo.io engineers.
oneLiner: Kubernetes-native AI agent platform using CRDs and AutoGen for DevOps automation and cluster management.
funding: Backed by Solo.io ($56M+ total). Open-source project from Solo.io engineers.
pricing: Open Source (Apache 2.0, free). No commercial tier.
capabilities: Web UI for management and chat

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: K8s-native: agents defined as CRDs via kubectl or web UI. Specs declare LLM provider, system prompt, tools, permissions in YAML. Web UI provides chat interface and management console. Pre-built templates for DevOps scenarios. Assumes K8s familiarity.
strengths: True K8s-native — agents are first-class K8s resources
CRD definitions are GitOps-friendly and version-controllable
Leverages K8s RBAC for agent permissions
Deep DevOps tool integrations (kubectl, Helm, Istio, Prometheus)
weaknesses: Steep learning curve outside K8s teams
notableFeatures: CRD-based agent lifecycle via K8s controller pattern
Automatic K8s RBAC inheritance for agents
Istio/service mesh awareness for microservices debugging

recentDevelopments: 2025 Q1: Initial open-source release under Apache 2.0
2025 Q1: Pre-built tools for kubectl, Helm, Istio, Argo CD, Prometheus
2025 Q1: Web UI for agent management and chat

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: CRD YAML definitions + web UI. Familiar to K8s users, steep for others.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Web UI shows conversations and tool calls. No dedicated debugging tools.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: kagent.dev covers install and basics. Young project, still maturing.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: CRD updates require controller reconciliation. No hot-reload.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: AutoGen multi-agent conversations. CRDs define multiple collaborating agents.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Web UI chat interface. Basic AutoGen HITL. No structured handoff.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: CRDs are GitOps-friendly. No built-in team workspaces or promotion pipelines.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Pre-built agent definitions for K8s tasks. Limited library.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: 6-10 cloud-native tools. Deep within K8s. No business system connectors.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: K8s API primary. CRDs for config. AutoGen agent protocol. No MCP.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: K8s RBAC and ServiceAccount-based. No enterprise IAM beyond K8s.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: No RAG or knowledge base. Live system queries via tools only.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Delete CRDs to stop. K8s resource limits apply. No behavioral guardrails.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: AutoGen HITL. CRD approval configs. Web UI for review.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: K8s audit logging. Conversation history. No tamper-proof or compliance certs.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: K8s RBAC for platform access. Namespace isolation. No custom agent-level roles.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: True K8s-native: Helm chart, CRDs, runs as controller. Core differentiator. K8s-only though.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: K8s native scaling. Controller pattern inherently scalable. No benchmarks.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Prometheus integration. K8s observability. Web UI for conversations.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: ~1.5K GitHub stars. Young project. Growing cloud-native community.

scoringRationale: Kagent scores 52/100. Deployment (14) is standout — K8s-native CRD approach is unique differentiator. Integration (11) deep but narrow. Builder (10), Governance (10), Collaboration (8) limited as early project. Ecosystem (5) young with ~1.5K stars.
targetUser: Platform engineering teams automating Kubernetes operations

metrics.value: kubectl, Helm, Istio, Argo CD, Prometheus

walkthrough.title: How Kagent Works: Kubernetes-Native AI Agent for DevOps

steps.title: Install via Helm Chart
steps.description: Deploy Kagent to your Kubernetes cluster with a single Helm install command. The operator registers custom resource definitions (CRDs) for agent management. No external dependencies beyond the cluster.

steps.title: Define Agents Declaratively
steps.description: Create agents using Kubernetes YAML manifests — just like any other K8s resource. Specify the agent's capabilities (kubectl, Helm, Istio, Argo CD, Prometheus), LLM backend, and RBAC permissions. Apply with kubectl.

steps.title: Chat with Your Cluster
steps.description: Open the Kagent web UI and interact with your agents conversationally. Ask 'Why is pod X crashing?' or 'Deploy service Y to staging.' The agent uses kubectl, queries Prometheus metrics, and checks Helm releases to answer.

steps.title: Agent Diagnoses and Remediates
steps.description: When issues arise, the agent runs diagnostic commands (describe pods, check events, query metrics), correlates findings across tools, and proposes remediation. For approved operations, it applies fixes directly to the cluster.

steps.title: GitOps-Compatible Management
steps.description: Agent definitions are standard YAML — store them in Git, manage via Argo CD, and version control your agent fleet. K8s RBAC controls what each agent can access, ensuring least-privilege security.

youtubeReviews.title: KAgent: Run AI Agents on Kubernetes, No Cloud
